text: During a laparoscopic ovarian cystectomy, the surgeon performs hydrodissection of the left ovary using a vasopressin and normal saline solution to separate the ovarian cyst from the ovarian cortex. A monopolar energy device is used to incise the ovarian cortex, followed by graspers to separate the ovarian cortex from the hemorrhagic cyst. The cyst, containing encapsulated old blood, is successfully detached from the ovary without complications. Vasopressin aids in hemostasis, with a bipolar instrument available for additional hemostatic control if needed.

The surgeon performs hydrodissection of the right ovary using a laparoscopic needle to inject a vasopressin and saline solution between the ovarian cortex and the ovarian cyst. An incision is made in the ovarian cortex, and the cortex is grasped to separate it from the right ovarian dermoid cyst. The cyst is then carefully removed from the ovary.
The surgeon shells out the right ovarian dermoid cyst from the ovary, utilizing the vasopressin dilution to aid in hemostasis and facilitate the dissection process.
